'''Ōyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime''' are two {{ill|Kami|ja|神道の神|lt=Shinto deities}} appearing in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythological]] [[deity (Shinto)|female deities]]. In one book of the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], they are recorded as the daughters of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊|lt=Susanoo}} (father) and {{ill|Iomotowake|ja|五十猛命|lt=Iomotowake Hiko-no-Mikoto}} (brother). The sister goddess Ōyatsuhime is referred to as {{ill|Ōyatsuhime-no-Kami|ja|大屋都比売神|lt=Ōyatsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|Ōyatsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|大屋津姫命|lt=Ōyatsuhime-no-Mikoto}}, and the sister goddess Tsumatsuhime is referred to as {{ill|Tsumatsuhime-no-Kami|ja|抓津姫神|lt=Tsumatsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|抓津姫命|lt=Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Tsumamatu-hime-no-Kami|ja|都麻都比売命|lt=Tsumamatu-hime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|Tsumatsu-hime-no-Kami|ja|爪津姫神|lt=Tsumatsu-hime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|Tsumatsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|枛津姫神|lt=Tsumatsu-hime-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|Tsumatsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|枛津姫命|lt=Tsumatsu-hime-no-Mikoto}}, among others. Together with {{ill|Iomotowake|ja|五十猛命|lt=Iomotowake Hiko-no-Mikoto}}, under the command of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊|lt=Susanoo}}, they scattered tree species across all the mountains nationwide and returned to {{ill|Kii Province|ja|紀伊国|lt=Kii Province}} (now {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|lt=Wakayama Prefecture}}). == Overview == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|170px|{{Center|The first five generations of the [[Moriya Clan|守矢氏]] in 『守矢氏系譜』 (from Mote Yago to Hachikushi Kami)}}]] In the folk traditions of the {{ill|Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|lt=Suwa region}} ([[ill|Suwa faith|ja|諏訪信仰|lt=Suwa faith}}), Ōyatsuhime is considered the offspring deity of {{ill|Mote Yago|ja|洩矢神|lt=Mote Yago}}, or an alias of that deity. She is also equated with {{ill|Take Minakata|ja|建御名方神|lt=Take Minakata}}'s offspring deity {{ill|Uchi Prefecture Kami|ja|内県神|lt=Uchi Prefecture Kami}}. According to the 『Shuya Clan Genealogy』 (『守矢氏系譜』), established in the early Meiji period, Ōyatsuhime is the child of {{ill|Mote Takami|ja|洩宅神|lt=Mote Takami}} (son of {{ill|Mote Yago|ja|洩矢神|lt=Mote Yago}}) and is counted as the third generation of the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏|lt=Moriya Clan}} who inherited ceremonial duties. Her name is said to originate from when her father hunted deer and captured 1,000 deer.
Mote Takami was born with extraordinary spiritual powers, took over his father's duties, carried bow and arrows, followed the Great Deity in hunting and game, and obtained a thousand deer. He had one son and named him Chikatō Kami. Chikatō Kami succeeded in overseeing ceremonial affairs. (Excerpt) Ancient [[Kagura|kagura]] songs: :Chikatō no Kitano Hayashinowa Susumushiwa Susumushiwa Yachiyo no Koyete Tsunenitaisenu :Chikatō no Myōjin Shiyushūreshito Ohosoransu Ohosoransu Yukitataima no Hana no Kiyomeyo Chikatō's shrines are located in the Suwa District's Nakatsusa: Aruga, Uehara, Hanahara, Yokobuki, Kyudo in the Higashichikuma District's Kanda and Hayashi. Situated in {{ill|Urakoyama|ja|宇良古山|lt=Urakoyama}}, there exists a shrine dedicated to him. In ancient times, he was the deity of over thirty villages within the district. His consort is {{ill|Uragokohime-no-Mikoto|ja|宇良古比売命|lt=Uragokohime-no-Mikoto}}. According to oral tradition, there is a shrine of him in the same area. {{ill|Kodama Hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|lt=Kodama Hiko-no-Mikoto}}, the offspring of the Great Deity's child {{ill|Katakura Hade-no-Mikoto|ja|片倉辺命|lt=Katakura Hade-no-Mikoto}}, is considered the fourth generation of the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏|lt=Moriya Clan}}. He married {{ill|Mitasama Hime-no-Kami|ja|美都多麻比売神|lt=Mitasama Hime-no-Kami}}, the child of {{ill|Moritatsu-no-Kami|ja|守達神|lt=Moritatsu-no-Kami}}, and gave birth to {{ill|Hachikushi|ja|八櫛神|lt=Hachikushi}}.Suwa Education Association. "『守矢氏系譜』" 『Suwa Historical Documents Collection, Volume 28』, 1938, pp. 31-72.Nomoto Mikato. "Approach to Chikatō Kami" 『Study on the Emergence and Development of Suwa Faith』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2018, pp. 333-336.Imai Nohiku. "O Suwa-sama Faith and Kami no Naga" 『Gods' Village: Ancient Suwa Stories』, Kokusho Publishing, 1976, pp. 117-118.
According to the 『Genealogy』, Chikatō Kami married {{ill|Uragokohime-no-Mikoto|ja|宇良古比売命|lt=Uragokohime-no-Mikoto}} and moved to {{ill|Urakoyama|ja|宇良古山|lt=Urakoyama}} (now {{ill|Matsumoto City|ja|松本市|lt=Matsumoto City}}, {{ill|Kanda, Matsumoto|ja|神田|lt=Kanda}}), which suggests that Chikatō Kami was either exiled from Suwa or left voluntarily. It is also believed that the succession of Chikatō Kami by {{ill|Take Minakata|ja|建御名方神|lt=Take Minakata}}'s grandchild {{ill|Kodama Hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|lt=Kodama Hiko-no-Mikoto}} indicates that the {{ill|Suwa Clan|ja|諏訪氏|lt=Suwa Clan}} (descendants of the mythological {{ill|Take Minakata|ja|建御名方神|lt=Take Minakata}}) took over the local worship duties of indigenous forces. There are also traditions where the foreign-born Chikatō Kami married local daughters not only in Matsumoto but also in other places. A famous tale narrates that "Chikato Kami" from Shinshu came to {{ill|Akagiyama Shrine|ja|赤城神社|lt=Akagiyama Shrine}} and married the sister of the {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神|lt=Akagi Shrine}}.Imai Nohiku. "Chikato-sama" 『Study on the Emergence and Development of Suwa Faith』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2018, p.325.Nomoto Mikato. "Approach to Chikatō Kami" 『Study on the Emergence and Development of Suwa Faith』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2018, p.342.. == Faith == According to Imai Nohiku's research on the distribution of {{ill|Mishagujishin|ja|ミシャグジ信仰|lt=Mishagujishin}}, Chikatō Kami shrines are found not only in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|lt=Nagano Prefecture}} (13 shrines) but also in {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture}} (8 shrines), {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|lt=Saitama Prefecture}} (12 shrines), {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|lt=Gunma Prefecture}} (20 shrines), {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}} (12 shrines), {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture}} (7 shrines), and {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}} (15 shrines), spreading from Kanto to Tohoku. They have various representations such as "Chikatō," "Chikata," "Chikato," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," and "Chikata," with pronunciations including "Chikata," "Chikatsu," "Chikato (u)," etc.Imai Nohiku. "Chikatō Kami Approach" 『Study on the Emergence and Development of Suwa Faith』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2018, p.326.Nomoto Mikato. "Approach to Chikatō Kami" 『Study on the Emergence and Development of Suwa Faith』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2018, p.343.. The distribution of Chikatō Kami shrines is characterized by their apparent movement along mountainous regions (such as the [[Yatsugatake|八ヶ岳]], [[Haruna Mountain|榛名山]], [[Akagi Mountain|赤城山]], [[Mount Ontake|男体山]], [[Yamigomezaki|八溝山]]) and is believed to have spread through the eastward expansion of hunting clans (descendants of the [[Moriya Clan|守矢族]], the so-called [[Mote Yago|洩矢族]]). As deities of hunting, they are closely related to {{ill|Mishagujishin|ja|ミシャグジ信仰|lt=Mishagujishin}} and {{ill|Tenpaku Faith|ja|天白信仰|lt=Tenpaku Faith}}. According to Imai's research, in Yamanashi, Saitama, and Gunma, {{ill|Mishagujishin|ja|ミシャグジ信仰|lt=Mishagujishin}} shrines coincide with Chikatō Kami shrines.Yamato Iwao. "Shinano Ancient History Study" 『Shinano Ancient History Study』, Meicho Publishing, 1990, pp.201-202.Imai Nohiku. "Survey Collection of Shrine Priests" 『Study on Ancient Suwa and Mishagujis' Ceremonial Systems』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2017, pp.212-281.. In addition to hunting deities from Suwa (Shinshu), there are regions where Chikatō Kami is also revered as a deity of agriculture and fire prevention, similar to {{ill|Mishagujishin|ja|ミシャグジ信仰|lt=Mishagujishin}} and {{ill|Tenpaku Faith|ja|天白信仰|lt=Tenpaku Faith}}, with many shrines worshiping them as {{ill|Ishibō|ja|石棒|lt=Ishibō}} deities.Nomoto Mikato. "Approach to Chikatō Kami" 『Study on the Emergence and Development of Suwa Faith』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2018, pp.347-349.Imai Nohiku. "Survey of Shrine Priests" 『Study on Ancient Suwa and Mishagujis' Ceremonial Systems』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2017, pp.300-301.Imai Nohiku. "Survey of Shrine Priests" 『Study on Ancient Suwa and Mishagujis' Ceremonial Systems』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2017, pp.338-339.Tanaka Moto. "Primitive Agricultural Ritual Elements in Mote Yashima Ceremonial System" 『Study on Ancient Suwa and Mishagujis' Ceremonial Systems』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2017, p.192.. Moreover, many shrines have different main deities depending on the region, even within {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}. There are still many unexplored shrine precincts and regions, leaving numerous mysteries unresolved. === Main Shrines === ==== Koshin Region ==== * {{ill|Kanda Chikatō Shrine|ja|神田千鹿頭神社|lt=Kanda Chikatō Shrine}}, {{ill|Hayashi Chikatō Shrine|ja|林千鹿頭神社|lt=Hayashi Chikatō Shrine}} ({{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}, {{ill|Matsumoto City|ja|松本市|lt=Matsumoto City}} - Hataiwa) * {{ill|Chikatō Shrine|ja|千鹿頭神社|lt=Chikatō Shrine}} ({{ill|Suwa City, Nagano|ja|諏訪市|lt=Suwa City}}, Toyoda Aruga) * {{ill|Chikatō Shrine|ja|千鹿頭神社|lt=Chikatō Shrine}} ({{ill|Chino City|ja|茅野市|lt=Chino City}}, Chino Uehara) * {{ill|Chikatō Shrine|ja|千鹿頭神社|lt=Chikatō Shrine}} ({{ill|Chino City|ja|茅野市|lt=Chino City}}, Yoneda Hanahara) * {{ill|Chikatō Shrine|ja|千鹿頭神社|lt=Chikatō Shrine}} ({{ill|Yokobuki, Suwa City|ja|横吹|lt=Yokobuki}}, Suwa City) * {{ill|Chikatō Shrine|ja|千鹿頭神社|lt=Chikatō Shrine}} ({{ill|Higashiina District, Ichigata Town|ja|上伊那郡|lt=Higashiina District, Ichigata Town}}, Higashiina District, Ichigata Town) * {{ill|Chikata Shrine|ja|近津神社|lt=Chikata Shrine}} ({{ill|Kofu City, Yamanashi|ja|甲府市|lt=Kofu City}}, Kiyatecho) * {{ill|Chikata Shrine|ja|近戸神社|lt=Chikata Shrine}} ({{ill|Kofu City, Yamanashi|ja|甲府市|lt=Kofu City}}, Takamachi) ==== Kanto Region ==== * {{ill|Oogo Shrine|ja|大胡神社|lt=Oogo Shrine}} (Former {{ill|Chikata Shrine|ja|近戸神社|lt=Chikata Shrine}}) ({{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市|lt=Maebashi City}}, Kawaharaha) * {{ill|Chikata Shrine|ja|近戸神社|lt=Chikata Shrine}} ({{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kasukawa Town|ja|粕川町|lt=Kasukawa Town}}) * {{ill|Chikata Shrine|ja|近戸神社|lt=Chikata Shrine}} ({{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Battoi Town|ja|笂井町|lt=Battoi Town}}) * {{ill|Oyato Shrine|ja|親都神社|lt=Oyato Shrine}} ({{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Agatsuma District, Nakanojō Town|ja|吾妻郡|lt=Agatsuma District, Nakanojō Town}}) * {{ill|Chihō Shrine (Kazo Central)|ja|千方神社}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kazo City|ja|加須市|lt=Kazo City}}) * {{ill|Chishō Shrine (Kuki City's Kamijo)|ja|千勝社}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kuki City|ja|久喜市|lt=Kuki City}} - '''Ōyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime''' are two {{ill|Kami|ja|神道の神|lt=Shinto deities}} appearing in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythological]] [[deity (Shinto)|female deities]]. In one book of the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], they are recorded as the daughters of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊|lt=Susanoo}} (father) and {{ill|Iomotowake|ja|五十猛命|lt=Iomotowake Hiko-no-Mikoto}} (brother). The sister goddess Ōyatsuhime is referred to as {{ill|Ōyatsuhime-no-Kami|ja|大屋都比売神|lt=Ōyatsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|Ōyatsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|大屋津姫命|lt=Ōyatsuhime-no-Mikoto}}, and the sister goddess Tsumatsuhime is referred to as {{ill|Tsumatsuhime-no-Kami|ja|抓津姫神|lt=Tsumatsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|抓津姫命|lt=Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Tsumamatu-hime-no-Kami|ja|都麻都比売命|lt=Tsumamatu-hime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|Tsumatsu-hime-no-Kami|ja|爪津姫神|lt=Tsumatsu-hime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|Tsumatsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|枛津姫神|lt=Tsumatsu-hime-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|Tsumatsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|枛津姫命|lt=Tsumatsu-hime-no-Mikoto}}, among others. "O Suwa-sama Faith and Kami no Naga" 『Gods' Village: Ancient Suwa Stories』, Kokusho Publishing, 1976, pp. 117-118.
According to the 『Genealogy』, Chikatō Kami married {{ill|Uragokohime-no-Mikoto|ja|宇良古比売命|lt=Uragokohime-no-Mikoto}} and moved to {{ill|Urakoyama|ja|宇良古山|lt=Urakoyama}} (now {{ill|Matsumoto City|ja|松本市|lt=Matsumoto City}}, {{ill|Kanda, Matsumoto|ja|神田|lt=Kanda}}), which suggests that Chikatō Kami was either exiled from Suwa or left voluntarily. It is also believed that the succession of Chikatō Kami by {{ill|Take Minakata|ja|建御名方神|lt=Take Minakata}}'s grandchild {{ill|Kodama Hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|lt=Kodama Hiko-no-Mikoto}} indicates that the {{ill|Suwa Clan|ja|諏訪氏|lt=Suwa Clan}} (descendants of the mythological {{ill|Take Minakata|ja|建御名方神|lt=Take Minakata}}) took over the local worship duties of indigenous forces. There are also traditions where the foreign-born Chikatō Kami married local daughters not only in Matsumoto but also in other places. A famous tale narrates that "Chikato Kami" from Shinshu came to {{ill|Akagiyama Shrine|ja|赤城神社|lt=Akagiyama Shrine}} and married the sister of the {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神|lt=Akagi Shrine}}.Imai Nohiku. "Chikato-sama" 『Study on the Emergence and Development of Suwa Faith』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2018, p.325.Nomoto Mikato. "Approach to Chikatō Kami" 『Study on the Emergence and Development of Suwa Faith』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2018, p.342.. == Faith == According to Imai Nohiku's research on the distribution of {{ill|Mishagujishin|ja|ミシャグジ信仰|lt=Mishagujishin}}, Chikatō Kami shrines are found not only in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|lt=Nagano Prefecture}} (13 shrines) but also in {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture}} (8 shrines), {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|lt=Saitama Prefecture}} (12 shrines), {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|lt=Gunma Prefecture}} (20 shrines), {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}} (12 shrines), {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture}} (7 shrines), and {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}} (15 shrines), spreading from Kanto to Tohoku. They have various representations such as "Chikatō," "Chikata," "Chikato," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," and "Chikata," with pronunciations including "Chikata," "Chikatsu," "Chikato (u)," etc.Imai Nohiku. "Chikatō Kami Approach" 『Study on the Emergence and Development of Suwa Faith』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2018, p.326.Nomoto Mikato. "Approach to Chikatō Kami" 『Study on the Emergence and Development of Suwa Faith』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2018, p.343.. The distribution of Chikatō Kami shrines is characterized by their apparent movement along mountainous regions (such as the [[Yatsugatake|八ヶ岳]], [[Haruna Mountain|榛名山]], [[Akagi Mountain|赤城山]], [[Mount Ontake|男体山]], [[Yamigomezaki|八溝山]]) and is believed to have spread through the eastward expansion of hunting clans (descendants of the [[Moriya Clan|守矢族]], the so-called [[Mote Yago|洩矢族]]). As deities of hunting, they are closely related to {{ill|Mishagujishin|ja|ミシャグジ信仰|lt=Mishagujishin}} and {{ill|Tenpaku Faith|ja|天白信仰|lt=Tenpaku Faith}}. According to Imai's research, in Yamanashi, Saitama, and Gunma, {{ill|Mishagujishin|ja|ミシャグジ信仰|lt=Mishagujishin}} shrines coincide with Chikatō Kami shrines.Yamato Iwao. "Shinano Ancient History Study" 『Shinano Ancient History Study』, Meicho Publishing, 1990, pp.201-202.Imai Nohiku. "Survey Collection of Shrine Priests" 『Study on Ancient Suwa and Mishagujis' Ceremonial Systems』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2017, pp.212-281.. In addition to hunting deities from Suwa (Shinshu), there are regions where Chikatō Kami is also revered as a deity of agriculture and fire prevention, similar to {{ill|Mishagujishin|ja|ミシャグジ信仰|lt=Mishagujishin}} and {{ill|Tenpaku Faith|ja|天白信仰|lt=Tenpaku Faith}}, with many shrines worshiping them as {{ill|Ishibō|ja|石棒|lt=Ishibō}} deities.Nomoto Mikato. "Approach to Chikatō Kami" 『Study on the Emergence and Development of Suwa Faith』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2018, pp.347-349.Imai Nohiku. "Survey of Shrine Priests" 『Study on Ancient Suwa and Mishagujis' Ceremonial Systems』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2017, pp.300-301.Imai Nohiku. "Survey of Shrine Priests" 『Study on Ancient Suwa and Mishagujis' Ceremonial Systems』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2017, pp.338-339.Tanaka Moto. "Primitive Agricultural Ritual Elements in Mote Yashima Ceremonial System" 『Study on Ancient Suwa and Mishagujis' Ceremonial Systems』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2017, p.192.. Moreover, many shrines have different main deities depending on the region, even within {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}. There are still many unexplored shrine precincts and regions, leaving numerous mysteries unresolved. === Main Shrines === ==== Koshin Region ==== * {{ill|Kanda Chikatō Shrine|ja|神田千鹿頭神社|lt=Kanda Chikatō Shrine}}, {{ill|Hayashi Chikatō Shrine|ja|林千鹿頭神社|lt=Hayashi Chikatō Shrine}} ({{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}, {{ill|Matsumoto City|ja|松本市|lt=Matsumoto City}} - Hataiwa) * {{ill|Chikatō Shrine|ja|千鹿頭神社|lt=Chikatō Shrine}} ({{ill|Suwa City, Nagano|ja|諏訪市|lt=Suwa City}}, Toyoda Aruga) * {{ill|Chikatō Shrine|ja|千鹿頭神社|lt=Chikatō Shrine}} ({{ill|Chino City|ja|茅野市|lt=Chino City}}, Chino Uehara) * {{ill|Chikatō Shrine|ja|千鹿頭神社|lt=Chikatō Shrine}} ({{ill|Chino City|ja|茅野市|lt=Chino City}}, Yoneda Hanahara) * {{ill|Chikatō Shrine|ja|千鹿頭神社|lt=Chikatō Shrine}} ({{ill|Yokobuki, Suwa City|ja|横吹|lt=Yokobuki}}, Suwa City) * {{ill|Chikatō Shrine|ja|千鹿頭神社|lt=Chikatō Shrine}} ({{ill|Higashiina District, Ichigata Town|ja|上伊那郡|lt=Higashiina District, Ichigata Town}}, Higashiina District, Ichigata Town) * {{ill|Chikata Shrine|ja|近津神社|lt=Chikata Shrine}} ({{ill|Kofu City, Yamanashi|ja|甲府市|lt=Kofu City}}, Kiyatecho) * {{ill|Chikata Shrine|ja|近戸神社|lt=Chikata Shrine}} ({{ill|Kofu City, Yamanashi|ja|甲府市|lt=Kofu City}}, Takamachi) ==== Kanto Region ==== * {{ill|Oogo Shrine|ja|大胡神社|lt=Oogo Shrine}} (Former {{ill|Chikata Shrine|ja|近戸神社|lt=Chikata Shrine}}) ({{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市|lt=Maebashi City}}, Kawaharaha) * {{ill|Chikata Shrine|ja|近戸神社|lt=Chikata Shrine}} ({{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kasukawa Town|ja|粕川町|lt=Kasukawa Town}}) * {{ill|Chikata Shrine|ja|近戸神社|lt=Chikata Shrine}} ({{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Battoi Town|ja|笂井町|lt=Battoi Town}}) * {{ill|Oyato Shrine|ja|親都神社|lt=Oyato Shrine}} ({{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Agatsuma District, Nakanojō Town|ja|吾妻郡|lt=Agatsuma District, Nakanojō Town}}) * {{ill|Chihō Shrine (Kazo Central)|ja|千方神社}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kazo City|ja|加須市|lt=Kazo City}}) * {{ill|Chishō Shrine (Kuki City's Kamijo)|ja|千勝社}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kuki City|ja|久喜市|lt=Kuki City}} - Kamiji) * {{ill|Chishō Shrine (Kuki City's Honmachi)|ja|千勝神社}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kuki City|ja|久喜市|lt=Kuki City}} - Honmachi) * {{ill|Chishō Shrine (Kuki City's Yoshiba)|ja|千勝神社}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kuki City|ja|久喜市|lt=Kuki City}} - Yoshiba) * {{ill|Chikagata Shrine|ja|千形神社}} ({{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kumagaya City|ja|熊谷市|lt=Kumagaya City}} - Honmachi) * {{ill|Chigata Shrine|ja|智賀都神社}} ({{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya City|ja|宇都宮市|lt=Utsunomiya City}} - Tokujircho) * {{ill|Chihō Shrine|ja|智方神社}} ({{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Oyama City|ja|小山市|lt=Oyama City}} - Takei) * {{ill|Chiha Shrine|ja|血方神社}} ({{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Oyama City|ja|小山市|lt=Oyama City}} - Taman) * {{ill|Chikata Shrine (Kanda)|ja|近津神社}} ({{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Omiya City|ja|久喜市|lt=Omiya City}}) * {{ill|Chishō Shrine (Tsukubaji)|ja|千勝神社}} ({{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Tsukuba City|ja|つくば市|lt=Tsukuba City}} - Tomizaki) ==== Tohoku Region ==== * {{ill|Tudokohachishin Jinja|ja|都々古別神社|lt=Tudokohachishin Jinja}} - {{ill|Suwa District, Tobamachi|ja|東白川郡|lt=Suwa District, Tobamachi}} - Tobamachi * {{ill|Tudokohachishin Jinja|ja|都々古別神社|lt=Tudokohachishin Jinja}} ({{ill|Suwa District, Tashitsuki|ja|東白川郡棚倉町八槻|lt=Suwa District, Tashitsuki}}) - {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Iwanose Shrine|ja|岩峰山石神社|lt=Iwanose Shrine}} * {{ill|Hisayose Shrine (Chihashin Shrine)|ja|久来石神社}} ({{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}} - Hikoshi) == Footnotes == == References == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=『信州の文化シリーズ 寺と神社』|year=1981|publisher=Shinshu Mainichi Shimbunsha}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=『探訪 信州の古寺 天台宗・真言宗』|year=1996|publisher=Kyodo Publishing}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=『Shuya Clan Genealogy』|year=2017|editor=Ancient Tribe Research Society|publisher=Ningen-sha|isbn=978-4-908-62717-0|ref={{Harvid|Shuya Clan Genealogy|2017年}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=『Shinano Ancient History Study』|year=1990|editor=Yamato Iwao|publisher=Meicho Publishing|isbn=978-4-626-01363-7|ref={{Harvid|Shinano Ancient History Study|1990年}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=『Study on Ancient Suwa and Mishagujis' Ceremonial Systems』|year=2017|editor=Ancient Tribe Research Society|publisher=Ningen-sha|isbn=978-4-908-62716-3|ref={{Harvid|Study on Ancient Suwa and Mishagujis' Ceremonial Systems|2017年}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=『Study on Ancient Suwa and Mishagujis' Ceremonial Systems』|year=2017|editor=Ancient Tribe Research Society|publisher=Ningen-sha|isbn=978-4-908-62717-0|ref={{Harvid|Study on Ancient Suwa and Mishagujis' Ceremonial Systems|2018年}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=『Study on Mishagujishin』|year=2017|editor=Swanimism|title_addition= Issue 3 Special Feature: Mishaguji Restart}} == Related Topics == * [[List of Japanese deities|List of Japanese deities]] {{Shinto Navbox}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyatsuhime Tsumatsuhime}} [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Goddesses of vegetation]] [[Category:Shinto goddesses]] [[Category:Original pages]][[ja:オオヤツヒメ・ツマツヒメ]] {{User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru Faith}}

revision.text:
'''Ōyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime''' are two {{ill|Kami|ja|神道の神|lt=Shinto deities}} appearing in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythological]] [[deity (Shinto)|female deities]]. In one book of the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], they are recorded as the daughters of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊|lt=Susanoo}} (father) and {{ill|Iomotowake|ja|五十猛命|lt=Iomotowake Hiko-no-Mikoto}} (brother). The sister goddess Ōyatsuhime is referred to as {{ill|Ōyatsuhime-no-Kami|ja|大屋都比売神|lt=Ōyatsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|Ōyatsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|大屋津姫命|lt=Ōyatsuhime-no-Mikoto}}, and the sister goddess Tsumatsuhime is referred to as {{ill|Tsumatsuhime-no-Kami|ja|抓津姫神|lt=Tsumatsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|抓津姫命|lt=Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Tsumamatu-hime-no-Kami|ja|都麻都比売命|lt=Tsumamatu-hime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|Tsumatsu-hime-no-Kami|ja|爪津姫神|lt=Tsumatsu-hime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|Tsumatsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|枛津姫神|lt=Tsumatsu-hime-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|Tsumatsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|枛津姫命|lt=Tsumatsu-hime-no-Mikoto}}, among others. Together with {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Isonotakeru no mikoto|ja|五十猛命}}'''|五十猛命|}}, under the command of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊|lt=Susanoo}}, they scattered tree species across all the mountains nationwide and returned to {{ill|Kii Province|ja|紀伊国|lt=Kii Province}} (now {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|lt=Wakayama Prefecture}}). == Overview == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|170px|{{Center|The first five generations of the [[Moriya Clan|守矢氏]] in 『守矢氏系譜』 (from Mote Yago to Hachikushi Kami)}}]] In the folk traditions of the {{ill|Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|lt=Suwa region}} ([[ill|Suwa faith|ja|諏訪信仰|lt=Suwa faith}}), Ōyatsuhime is considered the offspring deity of {{ill|Mote Yago|ja|洩矢神|lt=Mote Yago}}, or an alias of that deity. She is also equated with {{ill|Take Minakata|ja|建御名方神|lt=Take Minakata}}'s offspring deity {{ill|Uchi Prefecture Kami|ja|内県神|lt=Uchi Prefecture Kami}}. According to the 『Shuya Clan Genealogy』 (『守矢氏系譜』), established in the early Meiji period, Ōyatsuhime is the child of {{ill|Mote Takami|ja|洩宅神|lt=Mote Takami}} (son of {{ill|Mote Yago|ja|洩矢神|lt=Mote Yago}}) and is counted as the third generation of the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏|lt=Moriya Clan}} who inherited ceremonial duties. Her name is said to originate from when her father hunted deer and captured 1,000 deer.
Mote Takami was born with extraordinary spiritual powers, took over his father's duties, carried bow and arrows, followed the Great Deity in hunting and game, and obtained a thousand deer. He had one son and named him Chikatō Kami. Chikatō Kami succeeded in overseeing ceremonial affairs. (Excerpt) Ancient [[Kagura|kagura]] songs: :Chikatō no Kitano Hayashinowa Susumushiwa Susumushiwa Yachiyo no Koyete Tsunenitaisenu :Chikatō no Myōjin Shiyushūreshito Ohosoransu Ohosoransu Yukitataima no Hana no Kiyomeyo Chikatō's shrines are located in the Suwa District's Nakatsusa: Aruga, Uehara, Hanahara, Yokobuki, Kyudo in the Higashichikuma District's Kanda and Hayashi. Situated in {{ill|Urakoyama|ja|宇良古山|lt=Urakoyama}}, there exists a shrine dedicated to him. In ancient times, he was the deity of over thirty villages within the district. His consort is {{ill|Uragokohime-no-Mikoto|ja|宇良古比売命|lt=Uragokohime-no-Mikoto}}. According to oral tradition, there is a shrine of him in the same area. {{ill|Kodama Hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|lt=Kodama Hiko-no-Mikoto}}, the offspring of the Great Deity's child {{ill|Katakura Hade-no-Mikoto|ja|片倉辺命|lt=Katakura Hade-no-Mikoto}}, is considered the fourth generation of the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏|lt=Moriya Clan}}. He married {{ill|Mitasama Hime-no-Kami|ja|美都多麻比売神|lt=Mitasama Hime-no-Kami}}, the child of {{ill|Moritatsu-no-Kami|ja|守達神|lt=Moritatsu-no-Kami}}, and gave birth to {{ill|Hachikushi|ja|八櫛神|lt=Hachikushi}}.Suwa Education Association. "『守矢氏系譜』" 『Suwa Historical Documents Collection, Volume 28』, 1938, pp. 31-72.Nomoto Mikato. "Approach to Chikatō Kami" 『Study on the Emergence and Development of Suwa Faith』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2018, pp. 333-336.Imai Nohiku. "O Suwa-sama Faith and Kami no Naga" 『Gods' Village: Ancient Suwa Stories』, Kokusho Publishing, 1976, pp. 117-118.

There are also traditions where the foreign-born Chikatō Kami married local daughters not only in Matsumoto but also in other places. Moreover, many shrines have different main deities depending on the region, even within {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}. There are still many unexplored shrine precincts and regions, leaving numerous mysteries unresolved. === Main Shrines === ==== Koshin Region ==== * {{ill|Kanda Chikatō Shrine|ja|神田千鹿頭神社|lt=Kanda Chikatō Shrine}}, {{ill|Hayashi Chikatō Shrine|ja|林千鹿頭神社|lt=Hayashi Chikatō Shrine}} ({{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}, {{ill|Matsumoto City|ja|松本市|lt=Matsumoto City}} - Hataiwa) * {{ill|Chikatō Shrine|ja|千鹿頭神社|lt=Chikatō Shrine}} ({{ill|Suwa City, Nagano|ja|諏訪市|lt=Suwa City}}, Toyoda Aruga) * {{ill|Chikatō Shrine|ja|千鹿頭神社|lt=Chikatō Shrine}} ({{ill|Chino City|ja|茅野市|lt=Chino City}}, Chino Uehara) * {{ill|Chikatō Shrine|ja|千鹿頭神社|lt=Chikatō Shrine}} ({{ill|Chino City|ja|茅野市|lt=Chino City}}, Yoneda Hanahara) * {{ill|Chikatō Shrine|ja|千鹿頭神社|lt=Chikatō Shrine}} ({{ill|Yokobuki, Suwa City|ja|横吹|lt=Yokobuki}}, Suwa City) * {{ill|Chikatō Shrine|ja|千鹿頭神社|lt=Chikatō Shrine}} ({{ill|Higashiina District, Ichigata Town|ja|上伊那郡|lt=Higashiina District, Ichigata Town}}, Higashiina District, Ichigata Town) * {{ill|Chikata Shrine|ja|近津神社|lt=Chikata Shrine}} ({{ill|Kofu City, Yamanashi|ja|甲府市|lt=Kofu City}}, Kiyatecho) * {{ill|Chikata Shrine|ja|近戸神社|lt=Chikata Shrine}} ({{ill|Kofu City, Yamanashi|ja|甲府市|lt=Kofu City}}, Takamachi) ==== Kanto Region ==== * {{ill|Oogo Shrine|ja|大胡神社|lt=Oogo Shrine}} (Former {{ill|Chikata Shrine|ja|近戸神社|lt=Chikata Shrine}}) ({{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市|lt=Maebashi City}}, Kawaharaha) * {{ill|Chikata Shrine|ja|近戸神社|lt=Chikata Shrine}} ({{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kasukawa Town|ja|粕川町|lt=Kasukawa Town}}) * {{ill|Chikata Shrine|ja|近戸神社|lt=Chikata Shrine}} ({{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Battoi Town|ja|笂井町|lt=Battoi Town}}) * {{ill|Oyato Shrine|ja|親都神社|lt=Oyato Shrine}} ({{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Agatsuma District, Nakanojō Town|ja|吾妻郡|lt=Agatsuma District, Nakanojō Town}}) * {{ill|Chihō Shrine (Kazo Central)|ja|千方神社}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kazo City|ja|加須市|lt=Kazo City}}) * {{ill|Chishō Shrine (Kuki City's Kamijo)|ja|千勝社}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kuki City|ja|久喜市|lt=Kuki City}} - Kamiji) * {{ill|Chishō Shrine (Kuki City's Honmachi)|ja|千勝神社}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kuki City|ja|久喜市|lt=Kuki City}} - Honmachi) * {{ill|Chishō Shrine (Kuki City's Yoshiba)|ja|千勝神社}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kuki City|ja|久喜市|lt=Kuki City}} - Yoshiba) * {{ill|Chikagata Shrine|ja|千形神社}} ({{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kumagaya City|ja|熊谷市|lt=Kumagaya City}} - Honmachi) * {{ill|Chigata Shrine|ja|智賀都神社}} ({{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya City|ja|宇都宮市|lt=Utsunomiya City}} - Tokujircho) * {{ill|Chihō Shrine|ja|智方神社}} ({{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Oyama City|ja|小山市|lt=Oyama City}} - Takei) * {{ill|Chiha Shrine|ja|血方神社}} ({{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Oyama City|ja|小山市|lt=Oyama City}} - Taman) * {{ill|Chikata Shrine (Kanda)|ja|近津神社}} ({{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Omiya City|ja|久喜市|lt=Omiya City}}) * {{ill|Chishō Shrine (Tsukubaji)|ja|千勝神社}} ({{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Tsukuba City|ja|つくば市|lt=Tsukuba City}} - Tomizaki) ==== Tohoku Region ==== * {{ill|Tudokohachishin Jinja|ja|都々古別神社|lt=Tudokohachishin Jinja}} - {{ill|Suwa District, Tobamachi|ja|東白川郡|lt=Suwa District, Tobamachi}} - Tobamachi * {{ill|Tudokohachishin Jinja|ja|都々古別神社|lt=Tudokohachishin Jinja}} ({{ill|Suwa District, Tashitsuki|ja|東白川郡棚倉町八槻|lt=Suwa District, Tashitsuki}}) - {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Iwanose Shrine|ja|岩峰山石神社|lt=Iwanose Shrine}} * {{ill|Hisayose Shrine (Chihashin Shrine)|ja|久来石神社}} ({{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}} - Hikoshi) == Footnotes == == References == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=『信州の文化シリーズ 寺と神社』|year=1981|publisher=Shinshu Mainichi Shimbunsha}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=『探訪 信州の古寺 天台宗・真言宗』|year=1996|publisher=Kyodo Publishing}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=『Shuya Clan Genealogy』|year=2017|editor=Ancient Tribe Research Society|publisher=Ningen-sha|isbn=978-4-908-62717-0|ref={{Harvid|Shuya Clan Genealogy|2017年}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=『Shinano Ancient History Study』|year=1990|editor=Yamato Iwao|publisher=Meicho Publishing|isbn=978-4-626-01363-7|ref={{Harvid|Shinano Ancient History Study|1990年}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=『Study on Ancient Suwa and Mishagujis' Ceremonial Systems』|year=2017|editor=Ancient Tribe Research Society|publisher=Ningen-sha|isbn=978-4-908-62716-3|ref={{Harvid|Study on Ancient Suwa and Mishagujis' Ceremonial Systems|2017年}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=『Study on Ancient Suwa and Mishagujis' Ceremonial Systems』|year=2017|editor=Ancient Tribe Research Society|publisher=Ningen-sha|isbn=978-4-908-62717-0|ref={{Harvid|Study on Ancient Suwa and Mishagujis' Ceremonial Systems|2018年}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=『Study on Mishagujishin』|year=2017|editor=Swanimism|title_addition= Issue 3 Special Feature: Mishaguji Restart}} == Related Topics == * [[List of Japanese deities|List of Japanese deities]] {{Shinto Navbox}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyatsuhime Tsumatsuhime}} [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Goddesses of vegetation]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Shinto goddesses]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Original pages]][[ja:オオヤツヒメ・ツマツヒメ]] {{User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru Faith}}

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:new pages]][[category:original pages]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Ōyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime''' are two {{ill|Kami|ja|神道の神|lt=Shinto deities}} appearing in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythological]] [[deity (Shinto)|female deities]]. In one book of the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], they are recorded as the daughters of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊|lt=Susanoo}} (father) and {{ill|Iomotowake|ja|五十猛命|lt=Iomotowake Hiko-no-Mikoto}} (brother). The sister goddess Ōyatsuhime is referred to as {{ill|Ōyatsuhime-no-Kami|ja|大屋都比売神|lt=Ōyatsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|Ōyatsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|大屋津姫命|lt=Ōyatsuhime-no-Mikoto}}, and the sister goddess Tsumatsuhime is referred to as {{ill|Tsumatsuhime-no-Kami|ja|抓津姫神|lt=Tsumatsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|抓津姫命|lt=Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Tsumamatu-hime-no-Kami|ja|都麻都比売命|lt=Tsumamatu-hime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|Tsumatsu-hime-no-Kami|ja|爪津姫神|lt=Tsumatsu-hime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|Tsumatsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|枛津姫神|lt=Tsumatsu-hime-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|Tsumatsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|枛津姫命|lt=Tsumatsu-hime-no-Mikoto}}, among others. Together with {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Isonotakeru no mikoto|ja|五十猛命}}'''|五十猛命|}}, under the command of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊|lt=Susanoo}}, they scattered tree species across all the mountains nationwide and returned to {{ill|Kii Province|ja|紀伊国|lt=Kii Province}} (now {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|lt=Wakayama Prefecture}}). == Overview == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|170px|{{Center|The first five generations of the [[Moriya Clan|守矢氏]] in 『守矢氏系譜』 (from Mote Yago to Hachikushi Kami)}}]] In the folk traditions of the {{ill|Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|lt=Suwa region}} ([[ill|Suwa faith|ja|諏訪信仰|lt=Suwa faith}}), Ōyatsuhime is considered the offspring deity of {{ill|Mote Yago|ja|洩矢神|lt=Mote Yago}}, or an alias of that deity. She is also equated with {{ill|Take Minakata|ja|建御名方神|lt=Take Minakata}}'s offspring deity {{ill|Uchi Prefecture Kami|ja|内県神|lt=Uchi Prefecture Kami}}. According to the 『Shuya Clan Genealogy』 (『守矢氏系譜』), established in the early Meiji period, Ōyatsuhime is the child of {{ill|Mote Takami|ja|洩宅神|lt=Mote Takami}} (son of {{ill|Mote Yago|ja|洩矢神|lt=Mote Yago}}) and is counted as the third generation of the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏|lt=Moriya Clan}} who inherited ceremonial duties. Her name is said to originate from when her father hunted deer and captured 1,000 deer.
Mote Takami was born with extraordinary spiritual powers, took over his father's duties, carried bow and arrows, followed the Great Deity in hunting and game, and obtained a thousand deer. He had one son and named him Chikatō Kami. Chikatō Kami succeeded in overseeing ceremonial affairs. (Excerpt) Ancient [[Kagura|kagura]] songs: :Chikatō no Kitano Hayashinowa Susumushiwa Susumushiwa Yachiyo no Koyete Tsunenitaisenu :Chikatō no Myōjin Shiyushūreshito Ohosoransu Ohosoransu Yukitataima no Hana no Kiyomeyo Chikatō's shrines are located in the Suwa District's Nakatsusa: Aruga, Uehara, Hanahara, Yokobuki, Kyudo in the Higashichikuma District's Kanda and Hayashi. Situated in {{ill|Urakoyama|ja|宇良古山|lt=Urakoyama}}, there exists a shrine dedicated to him. In ancient times, he was the deity of over thirty villages within the district. His consort is {{ill|Uragokohime-no-Mikoto|ja|宇良古比売命|lt=Uragokohime-no-Mikoto}}. According to oral tradition, there is a shrine of him in the same area. {{ill|Kodama Hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|lt=Kodama Hiko-no-Mikoto}}, the offspring of the Great Deity's child {{ill|Katakura Hade-no-Mikoto|ja|片倉辺命|lt=Katakura Hade-no-Mikoto}}, is considered the fourth generation of the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏|lt=Moriya Clan}}. He married {{ill|Mitasama Hime-no-Kami|ja|美都多麻比売神|lt=Mitasama Hime-no-Kami}}, the child of {{ill|Moritatsu-no-Kami|ja|守達神|lt=Moritatsu-no-Kami}}, and gave birth to {{ill|Hachikushi|ja|八櫛神|lt=Hachikushi}}.Suwa Education Association. "『守矢氏系譜』" 『Suwa Historical Documents Collection, Volume 28』, 1938, pp. 31-72.Nomoto Mikato. "Approach to Chikatō Kami" 『Study on the Emergence and Development of Suwa Faith』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2018, pp. 333-336.Imai Nohiku. "O Suwa-sama Faith and Kami no Naga" 『Gods' Village: Ancient Suwa Stories』, Kokusho Publishing, 1976, pp. 117-118.
According to the 『Genealogy』, Chikatō Kami married {{ill|Uragokohime-no-Mikoto|ja|宇良古比売命|lt=Uragokohime-no-Mikoto}} and moved to {{ill|Urakoyama|ja|宇良古山|lt=Urakoyama}} (now {{ill|Matsumoto City|ja|松本市|lt=Matsumoto City}}, {{ill|Kanda, Matsumoto|ja|神田|lt=Kanda}}), which suggests that Chikatō Kami was either exiled from Suwa or left voluntarily. It is also believed that the succession of Chikatō Kami by {{ill|Take Minakata|ja|建御名方神|lt=Take Minakata}}'s grandchild {{ill|Kodama Hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|lt=Kodama Hiko-no-Mikoto}} indicates that the {{ill|Suwa Clan|ja|諏訪氏|lt=Suwa Clan}} (descendants of the mythological {{ill|Take Minakata|ja|建御名方神|lt=Take Minakata}}) took over the local worship duties of indigenous forces. There are also traditions where the foreign-born Chikatō Kami married local daughters not only in Matsumoto but also in other places. A famous tale narrates that "Chikato Kami" from Shinshu came to {{ill|Akagiyama Shrine|ja|赤城神社|lt=Akagiyama Shrine}} and married the sister of the {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神|lt=Akagi Shrine}}.Imai Nohiku. "Chikato-sama" 『Study on the Emergence and Development of Suwa Faith』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2018, p.325.Nomoto Mikato. "Approach to Chikatō Kami" 『Study on the Emergence and Development of Suwa Faith』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2018, p.342.. == Faith == According to Imai Nohiku's research on the distribution of {{ill|Mishagujishin|ja|ミシャグジ信仰|lt=Mishagujishin}}, Chikatō Kami shrines are found not only in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|lt=Nagano Prefecture}} (13 shrines) but also in {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture}} (8 shrines), {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|lt=Saitama Prefecture}} (12 shrines), {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|lt=Gunma Prefecture}} (20 shrines), {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}} (12 shrines), {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture}} (7 shrines), and {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}} (15 shrines), spreading from Kanto to Tohoku. They have various representations such as "Chikatō," "Chikata," "Chikato," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," and "Chikata," with pronunciations including "Chikata," "Chikatsu," "Chikato (u)," etc.Imai Nohiku. "Chikatō Kami Approach" 『Study on the Emergence and Development of Suwa Faith』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2018, p.326.Nomoto Mikato. "Approach to Chikatō Kami" 『Study on the Emergence and Development of Suwa Faith』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2018, p.343.. The distribution of Chikatō Kami shrines is characterized by their apparent movement along mountainous regions (such as the [[Yatsugatake|八ヶ岳]], [[Haruna Mountain|榛名山]], [[Akagi Mountain|赤城山]], [[Mount Ontake|男体山]], [[Yamigomezaki|八溝山]]) and is believed to have spread through the eastward expansion of hunting clans (descendants of the [[Moriya Clan|守矢族]], the so-called [[Mote Yago|洩矢族]]). As deities of hunting, they are closely related to {{ill|Mishagujishin|ja|ミシャグジ信仰|lt=Mishagujishin}} and {{ill|Tenpaku Faith|ja|天白信仰|lt=Tenpaku Faith}}. According to Imai's research, in Yamanashi, Saitama, and Gunma, {{ill|Mishagujishin|ja|ミシャグジ信仰|lt=Mishagujishin}} shrines coincide with Chikatō Kami shrines.Yamato Iwao. "Shinano Ancient History Study" 『Shinano Ancient History Study』, Meicho Publishing, 1990, pp.201-202.Imai Nohiku. "Survey Collection of Shrine Priests" 『Study on Ancient Suwa and Mishagujis' Ceremonial Systems』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2017, pp.212-281.. In addition to hunting deities from Suwa (Shinshu), there are regions where Chikatō Kami is also revered as a deity of agriculture and fire prevention, similar to {{ill|Mishagujishin|ja|ミシャグジ信仰|lt=Mishagujishin}} and {{ill|Tenpaku Faith|ja|天白信仰|lt=Tenpaku Faith}}, with many shrines worshiping them as {{ill|Ishibō|ja|石棒|lt=Ishibō}} deities.Nomoto Mikato. "Approach to Chikatō Kami" 『Study on the Emergence and Development of Suwa Faith』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2018, pp.347-349.Imai Nohiku. "Survey of Shrine Priests" 『Study on Ancient Suwa and Mishagujis' Ceremonial Systems』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2017, pp.300-301.Imai Nohiku. "Survey of Shrine Priests" 『Study on Ancient Suwa and Mishagujis' Ceremonial Systems』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2017, pp.338-339.Tanaka Moto. "Primitive Agricultural Ritual Elements in Mote Yashima Ceremonial System" 『Study on Ancient Suwa and Mishagujis' Ceremonial Systems』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2017, p.192.. Moreover, many shrines have different main deities depending on the region, even within {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}. There are still many unexplored shrine precincts and regions, leaving numerous mysteries unresolved. === Main Shrines === ==== Koshin Region ==== * {{ill|Kanda Chikatō Shrine|ja|神田千鹿頭神社|lt=Kanda Chikatō Shrine}}, {{ill|Hayashi Chikatō Shrine|ja|林千鹿頭神社|lt=Hayashi Chikatō Shrine}} ({{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}, {{ill|Matsumoto City|ja|松本市|lt=Matsumoto City}} - Hataiwa) * {{ill|Chikatō Shrine|ja|千鹿頭神社|lt=Chikatō Shrine}} ({{ill|Suwa City, Nagano|ja|諏訪市|lt=Suwa City}}, Toyoda Aruga) * {{ill|Chikatō Shrine|ja|千鹿頭神社|lt=Chikatō Shrine}} ({{ill|Chino City|ja|茅野市|lt=Chino City}}, Chino Uehara) * {{ill|Chikatō Shrine|ja|千鹿頭神社|lt=Chikatō Shrine}} ({{ill|Chino City|ja|茅野市|lt=Chino City}}, Yoneda Hanahara) * {{ill|Chikatō Shrine|ja|千鹿頭神社|lt=Chikatō Shrine}} ({{ill|Yokobuki, Suwa City|ja|横吹|lt=Yokobuki}}, Suwa City) * {{ill|Chikatō Shrine|ja|千鹿頭神社|lt=Chikatō Shrine}} ({{ill|Higashiina District, Ichigata Town|ja|上伊那郡|lt=Higashiina District, Ichigata Town}}, Higashiina District, Ichigata Town) * {{ill|Chikata Shrine|ja|近津神社|lt=Chikata Shrine}} ({{ill|Kofu City, Yamanashi|ja|甲府市|lt=Kofu City}}, Kiyatecho) * {{ill|Chikata Shrine|ja|近戸神社|lt=Chikata Shrine}} ({{ill|Kofu City, Yamanashi|ja|甲府市|lt=Kofu City}}, Takamachi) ==== Kanto Region ==== * {{ill|Oogo Shrine|ja|大胡神社|lt=Oogo Shrine}} (Former {{ill|Chikata Shrine|ja|近戸神社|lt=Chikata Shrine}}) ({{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市|lt=Maebashi City}}, Kawaharaha) * {{ill|Chikata Shrine|ja|近戸神社|lt=Chikata Shrine}} ({{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kasukawa Town|ja|粕川町|lt=Kasukawa Town}}) * {{ill|Chikata Shrine|ja|近戸神社|lt=Chikata Shrine}} ({{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Battoi Town|ja|笂井町|lt=Battoi Town}}) * {{ill|Oyato Shrine|ja|親都神社|lt=Oyato Shrine}} ({{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Agatsuma District, Nakanojō Town|ja|吾妻郡|lt=Agatsuma District, Nakanojō Town}}) * {{ill|Chihō Shrine (Kazo Central)|ja|千方神社}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kazo City|ja|加須市|lt=Kazo City}}) * {{ill|Chishō Shrine (Kuki City's Kamijo)|ja|千勝社}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kuki City|ja|久喜市|lt=Kuki City}} - Kamiji) * {{ill|Chishō Shrine (Kuki City's Honmachi)|ja|千勝神社}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kuki City|ja|久喜市|lt=Kuki City}} - Honmachi) * {{ill|Chishō Shrine (Kuki City's Yoshiba)|ja|千勝神社}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kuki City|ja|久喜市|lt=Kuki City}} - Yoshiba) * {{ill|Chikagata Shrine|ja|千形神社}} ({{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kumagaya City|ja|熊谷市|lt=Kumagaya City}} - Honmachi) * {{ill|Chigata Shrine|ja|智賀都神社}} ({{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya City|ja|宇都宮市|lt=Utsunomiya City}} - Tokujircho) * {{ill|Chihō Shrine|ja|智方神社}} ({{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Oyama City|ja|小山市|lt=Oyama City}} - Takei) * {{ill|Chiha Shrine|ja|血方神社}} ({{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Oyama City|ja|小山市|lt=Oyama City}} - Hikoshi) == Footnotes == == References == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=『信州の文化シリーズ 寺と神社』|year=1981|publisher=Shinshu Mainichi Shimbunsha}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=『探訪 信州の古寺 天台宗・真言宗』|year=1996|publisher=Kyodo Publishing}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=『Shuya Clan Genealogy』|year=2017|editor=Ancient Tribe Research Society|publisher=Ningen-sha|isbn=978-4-908-62717-0|ref={{Harvid|Shuya Clan Genealogy|2017年}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=『Shinano Ancient History Study』|year=1990|editor=Yamato Iwao|publisher=Meicho Publishing|isbn=978-4-626-01363-7|ref={{Harvid|Shinano Ancient History Study|1990年}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=『Study on Ancient Suwa and Mishagujis' Ceremonial Systems』|year=2017|editor=Ancient Tribe Research Society|publisher=Ningen-sha|isbn=978-4-908-62716-3|ref={{Harvid|Study on Ancient Suwa and Mishagujis' Ceremonial Systems|2017年}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=『Study on Ancient Suwa and Mishagujis' Ceremonial Systems』|year=2017|editor=Ancient Tribe Research Society|publisher=Ningen-sha|isbn=978-4-908-62717-0|ref={{Harvid|Study on Ancient Suwa and Mishagujis' Ceremonial Systems|2018年}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=『Study on Mishagujishin』|year=2017|editor=Swanimism|title_addition= Issue 3 Special Feature: Mishaguji Restart}} == Related Topics == * [[List of Japanese deities|List of Japanese deities]] {{Shinto Navbox}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyatsuhime Tsumatsuhime}} [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Goddesses of vegetation]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Shinto goddesses]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:new pages]][[category:original pages]] [[ja:オオヤツヒメ・ツマツヒメ]] {{User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru Faith}}

'''Ōyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime''' are two {{ill|Kami|ja|神道の神|lt=Shinto deities}} appearing in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythological]] [[deity (Shinto)|female deities]]. In one book of the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], they are recorded as the daughters of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊|lt=Susanoo}} (father) and {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Isonotakeru no mikoto|ja|五十猛命}}'''|五十猛命|}} (brother). The sister goddess Ōyatsuhime is referred to as {{ill|Ōyatsuhime-no-Kami|ja|大屋都比売神|lt=Ōyatsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|Ōyatsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|大屋津姫命|lt=Ōyatsuhime-no-Mikoto}}, and the sister goddess Tsumatsuhime is referred to as {{ill|Tsumatsuhime-no-Kami|ja|抓津姫神|lt=Tsumatsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|抓津姫命|lt=Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Tsumamatu-hime-no-Kami|ja|都麻都比売命|lt=Tsumamatu-hime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|Tsumatsu-hime-no-Kami|ja|爪津姫神|lt=Tsumatsu-hime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|Tsumatsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|枛津姫神|lt=Tsumatsu-hime-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|Tsumatsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|枛津姫命|lt=Tsumatsu-hime-no-Mikoto}}, among others. Together with {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Isonotakeru no mikoto|ja|五十猛命}}'''|五十猛命|}}, under the command of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊|lt=Susanoo}}, they scattered tree species across all the mountains nationwide and returned to {{ill|Kii Province|ja|紀伊国|lt=Kii Province}} (now {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|lt=Wakayama Prefecture}}). == Overview == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|170px|{{Center|The first five generations of the [[Moriya Clan|守矢氏]] in 『守矢氏系譜』 (from Mote Yago to Hachikushi Kami)}}]] In the folk traditions of the {{ill|Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|lt=Suwa region}} ([[ill|Suwa faith|ja|諏訪信仰|lt=Suwa faith}}), Ōyatsuhime is considered the offspring deity of {{ill|Mote Yago|ja|洩矢神|lt=Mote Yago}}, or an alias of that deity. She is also equated with {{ill|Take Minakata|ja|建御名方神|lt=Take Minakata}}'s offspring deity {{ill|Uchi Prefecture Kami|ja|内県神|lt=Uchi Prefecture Kami}}. According to the 『Shuya Clan Genealogy』 (『守矢氏系譜』), established in the early Meiji period, Ōyatsuhime is the child of {{ill|Mote Takami|ja|洩宅神|lt=Mote Takami}} (son of {{ill|Mote Yago|ja|洩矢神|lt=Mote Yago}}) and is counted as the third generation of the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏|lt=Moriya Clan}} who inherited ceremonial duties. Her name is said to originate from when her father hunted deer and captured 1,000 deer.
Mote Takami was born with extraordinary spiritual powers, took over his father's duties, carried bow and arrows, followed the Great Deity in hunting and game, and obtained a thousand deer. He had one son and named him Chikatō Kami. Chikatō Kami succeeded in overseeing ceremonial affairs. (Excerpt) Ancient [[Kagura|kagura]] songs: :Chikatō no Kitano Hayashinowa Susumushiwa Susumushiwa Yachiyo no Koyete Tsunenitaisenu :Chikatō no Myōjin Shiyushūreshito Ohosoransu Ohosoransu Yukitataima no Hana no Kiyomeyo Chikatō's shrines are located in the Suwa District's Nakatsusa: Aruga, Uehara, Hanahara, Yokobuki, Kyudo in the Higashichikuma District's Kanda and Hayashi. Situated in {{ill|Urakoyama|ja|宇良古山|lt=Urakoyama}}, there exists a shrine dedicated to him. In ancient times, he was the deity of over thirty villages within the district. His consort is {{ill|Uragokohime-no-Mikoto|ja|宇良古比売命|lt=Uragokohime-no-Mikoto}}. According to oral tradition, there is a shrine of him in the same area. {{ill|Kodama Hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|lt=Kodama Hiko-no-Mikoto}}, the offspring of the Great Deity's child {{ill|Katakura Hade-no-Mikoto|ja|片倉辺命|lt=Katakura Hade-no-Mikoto}}, is considered the fourth generation of the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏|lt=Moriya Clan}}. He married {{ill|Mitasama Hime-no-Kami|ja|美都多麻比売神|lt=Mitasama Hime-no-Kami}}, the child of {{ill|Moritatsu-no-Kami|ja|守達神|lt=Moritatsu-no-Kami}}, and gave birth to {{ill|Hachikushi|ja|八櫛神|lt=Hachikushi}}.Suwa Education Association. "『守矢氏系譜』" 『Suwa Historical Documents Collection, Volume 28』, 1938, pp. 31-72.Nomoto Mikato. "Approach to Chikatō Kami" 『Study on the Emergence and Development of Suwa Faith』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2018, pp. 333-336.Imai Nohiku. "O Suwa-sama Faith and Kami no Naga" 『Gods' Village: Ancient Suwa Stories』, Kokusho Publishing, 1976, pp. 117-118.
According to the 『Genealogy』, Chikatō Kami married {{ill|Uragokohime-no-Mikoto|ja|宇良古比売命|lt=Uragokohime-no-Mikoto}} and moved to {{ill|Urakoyama|ja|宇良古山|lt=Urakoyama}} (now {{ill|Matsumoto City|ja|松本市|lt=Matsumoto City}}, {{ill|Kanda, Matsumoto|ja|神田|lt=Kanda}}), which suggests that Chikatō Kami was either exiled from Suwa or left voluntarily. It is also believed that the succession of Chikatō Kami by {{ill|Take Minakata|ja|建御名方神|lt=Take Minakata}}'s grandchild {{ill|Kodama Hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|lt=Kodama Hiko-no-Mikoto}} indicates that the {{ill|Suwa Clan|ja|諏訪氏|lt=Suwa Clan}} (descendants of the mythological {{ill|Take Minakata|ja|建御名方神|lt=Take Minakata}}) took over the local worship duties of indigenous forces. There are also traditions where the foreign-born Chikatō Kami married local daughters not only in Matsumoto but also in other places. A famous tale narrates that "Chikato Kami" from Shinshu came to {{ill|Akagiyama Shrine|ja|赤城神社|lt=Akagiyama Shrine}} and married the sister of the {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神|lt=Akagi Shrine}}.Imai Nohiku. "Chikato-sama" 『Study on the Emergence and Development of Suwa Faith』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2018, p.325.Nomoto Mikato. "Approach to Chikatō Kami" 『Study on the Emergence and Development of Suwa Faith』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2018, p.342.. == Faith == According to Imai Nohiku's research on the distribution of {{ill|Mishagujishin|ja|ミシャグジ信仰|lt=Mishagujishin}}, Chikatō Kami shrines are found not only in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|lt=Nagano Prefecture}} (13 shrines) but also in {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture}} (8 shrines), {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|lt=Saitama Prefecture}} (12 shrines), {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|lt=Gunma Prefecture}} (20 shrines), {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}} (12 shrines), {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture}} (7 shrines), and {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}} (15 shrines), spreading from Kanto to Tohoku. They have various representations such as "Chikatō," "Chikata," "Chikato," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," and "Chikata," with pronunciations including "Chikata," "Chikatsu," "Chikato (u)," etc.Imai Nohiku. "Chikatō Kami Approach" 『Study on the Emergence and Development of Suwa Faith』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2018, p.326.Nomoto Mikato. "Approach to Chikatō Kami" 『Study on the Emergence and Development of Suwa Faith』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2018, p.343.. The distribution of Chikatō Kami shrines is characterized by their apparent movement along mountainous regions (such as the [[Yatsugatake|八ヶ岳]], [[Haruna Mountain|榛名山]], [[Akagi Mountain|赤城山]], [[Mount Ontake|男体山]], [[Yamigomezaki|八溝山]]) and is believed to have spread through the eastward expansion of hunting clans (descendants of the [[Moriya Clan|守矢族]], the so-called [[Mote Yago|洩矢族]]). As deities of hunting, they are closely related to {{ill|Mishagujishin|ja|ミシャグジ信仰|lt=Mishagujishin}} and {{ill|Tenpaku Faith|ja|天白信仰|lt=Tenpaku Faith}}. According to Imai's research, in Yamanashi, Saitama, and Gunma, {{ill|Mishagujishin|ja|ミシャグジ信仰|lt=Mishagujishin}} shrines coincide with Chikatō Kami shrines.Yamato Iwao. "Shinano Ancient History Study" 『Shinano Ancient History Study』, Meicho Publishing, 1990, pp.201-202.Imai Nohiku. "Survey Collection of Shrine Priests" 『Study on Ancient Suwa and Mishagujis' Ceremonial Systems』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2017, pp.212-281.. In addition to hunting deities from Suwa (Shinshu), there are regions where Chikatō Kami is also revered as a deity of agriculture and fire prevention, similar to {{ill|Mishagujishin|ja|ミシャグジ信仰|lt=Mishagujishin}} and {{ill|Tenpaku Faith|ja|天白信仰|lt=Tenpaku Faith}}, with many shrines worshiping them as {{ill|Ishibō|ja|石棒|lt=Ishibō}} deities.Nomoto Mikato. "Approach to Chikatō Kami" 『Study on the Emergence and Development of Suwa Faith』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2018, pp.347-349.Imai Nohiku. "Survey of Shrine Priests" 『Study on Ancient Suwa and Mishagujis' Ceremonial Systems』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2017, pp.300-301.Imai Nohiku. "Survey of Shrine Priests" 『Study on Ancient Suwa and Mishagujis' Ceremonial Systems』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2017, pp.338-339.Tanaka Moto. "Primitive Agricultural Ritual Elements in Mote Yashima Ceremonial System" 『Study on Ancient Suwa and Mishagujis' Ceremonial Systems』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2017, p.192.. Moreover, many shrines have different main deities depending on the region, even within {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}. There are still many unexplored shrine precincts and regions, leaving numerous mysteries unresolved. === Main Shrines === ==== Koshin Region ==== * {{ill|Kanda Chikatō Shrine|ja|神田千鹿頭神社|lt=Kanda Chikatō Shrine}}, {{ill|Hayashi Chikatō Shrine|ja|林千鹿頭神社|lt=Hayashi Chikatō Shrine}} ({{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}, {{ill|Matsumoto City|ja|松本市|lt=Matsumoto City}} - Hataiwa) * {{ill|Chikatō Shrine|ja|千鹿頭神社|lt=Chikatō Shrine}} ({{ill|Suwa City, Nagano|ja|諏訪市|lt=Suwa City}}, Toyoda Aruga) * {{ill|Chikatō Shrine|ja|千鹿頭神社|lt=Chikatō Shrine}} ({{ill|Chino City|ja|茅野市|lt=Chino City}}, Chino Uehara) * {{ill|Chikatō Shrine|ja|千鹿頭神社|lt=Chikatō Shrine}} ({{ill|Chino City|ja|茅野市|lt=Chino City}}, Yoneda Hanahara) * {{ill|Chikatō Shrine|ja|千鹿頭神社|lt=Chikatō Shrine}} ({{ill|Yokobuki, Suwa City|ja|横吹|lt=Yokobuki}}, Suwa City) * {{ill|Chikatō Shrine|ja|千鹿頭神社|lt=Chikatō Shrine}} ({{ill|Higashiina District, Ichigata Town|ja|上伊那郡|lt=Higashiina District, Ichigata Town}}, Higashiina District, Ichigata Town) * {{ill|Chikata Shrine|ja|近津神社|lt=Chikata Shrine}} ({{ill|Kofu City, Yamanashi|ja|甲府市|lt=Kofu City}}, Kiyatecho) * {{ill|Chikata Shrine|ja|近戸神社|lt=Chikata Shrine}} ({{ill|Kofu City, Yamanashi|ja|甲府市|lt=Kofu City}}, Takamachi) ==== Kanto Region ==== * {{ill|Oogo Shrine|ja|大胡神社|lt=Oogo Shrine}} (Former {{ill|Chikata Shrine|ja|近戸神社|lt=Chikata Shrine}}) ({{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市|lt=Maebashi City}}, Kawaharaha) * {{ill|Chikata Shrine|ja|近戸神社|lt=Chikata Shrine}} ({{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kasukawa Town|ja|粕川町|lt=Kasukawa Town}}) * {{ill|Chikata Shrine|ja|近戸神社|lt=Chikata Shrine}} ({{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Battoi Town|ja|笂井町|lt=Battoi Town}}) * {{ill|Oyato Shrine|ja|親都神社|lt=Oyato Shrine}} ({{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Agatsuma District, Nakanojō Town|ja|吾妻郡|lt=Agatsuma District, Nakanojō Town}}) * {{ill|Chihō Shrine (Kazo Central)|ja|千方神社}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kazo City|ja|加須市|lt=Kazo City}}) * {{ill|Chishō Shrine (Kuki City's Kamijo)|ja|千勝社}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kuki City|ja|久喜市|lt=Kuki City}} - Kamiji) * {{ill|Chishō Shrine (Kuki City's Honmachi)|ja|千勝神社}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kuki City|ja|久喜市|lt=Kuki City}} - Honmachi) * {{ill|Chishō Shrine (Kuki City's Yoshiba)|ja|千勝神社}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kuki City|ja|久喜市|lt=Kuki City}} - Yoshiba) * {{ill|Chikagata Shrine|ja|千形神社}} ({{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kumagaya City|ja|熊谷市|lt=Kumagaya City}} - Honmachi) * {{ill|Chigata Shrine|ja|智賀都神社}} ({{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya City|ja|宇都宮市|lt=Utsunomiya City}} - Tokujircho) * {{ill|Chihō Shrine|ja|智方神社}} ({{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Oyama City|ja|小山市|lt=Oyama City}} - Takei) * {{ill|Chiha Shrine|ja|血方神社}} ({{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Oyama City|ja|小山市|lt=Oyama City}} - '''Ōyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime''' are two {{ill|Kami|ja|神道の神|lt=Shinto deities}} appearing in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythological]] [[deity (Shinto)|female deities]]. In one book of the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], they are recorded as the daughters of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊|lt=Susanoo}} (father) and {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Isonotakeru no mikoto|ja|五十猛命}}'''|五十猛命|}} (brother). The sister goddess Ōyatsuhime is referred to as {{ill|Ōyatsuhime-no-Kami|ja|大屋都比売神|lt=Ōyatsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|Ōyatsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|大屋津姫命|lt=Ōyatsuhime-no-Mikoto}}, and the sister goddess Tsumatsuhime is referred to as {{ill|Tsumatsuhime-no-Kami|ja|抓津姫神|lt=Tsumatsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|抓津姫命|lt=Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Tsumamatu-hime-no-Kami|ja|都麻都比売命|lt=Tsumamatu-hime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|Tsumatsu-hime-no-Kami|ja|爪津姫神|lt=Tsumatsu-hime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|Tsumatsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|枛津姫神|lt=Tsumatsu-hime-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|Tsumatsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|枛津姫命|lt=Tsumatsu-hime-no-Mikoto}}, among others. Together with {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Isonotakeru no mikoto|ja|五十猛命}}'''|五十猛命|}}, under the command of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊|lt=Susanoo}}, they scattered tree species across all the mountains nationwide and returned to {{ill|Kii Province|ja|紀伊国|lt=Kii Province}} (now {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|lt=Wakayama Prefecture}}). == Overview == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|170px|{{Center|The first five generations of the [[Moriya Clan|守矢氏]] in 『守矢氏系譜』 (from Mote Yago to Hachikushi Kami)}}]] In the folk traditions of the {{ill|Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|lt=Suwa region}} ([[ill|Suwa faith|ja|諏訪信仰|lt=Suwa faith}}), Ōyatsuhime is considered the offspring deity of {{ill|Mote Yago|ja|洩矢神|lt=Mote Yago}}, or an alias of that deity. She is also equated with {{ill|Take Minakata|ja|建御名方神|lt=Take Minakata}}'s offspring deity {{ill|Uchi Prefecture Kami|ja|内県神|lt=Uchi Prefecture Kami}}. According to the 『Shuya Clan Genealogy』 (『守矢氏系譜』), established in the early Meiji period, Ōyatsuhime is the child of {{ill|Mote Takami|ja|洩宅神|lt=Mote Takami}} (son of {{ill|Mote Yago|ja|洩矢神|lt=Mote Yago}}) and is counted as the third generation of the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏|lt=Moriya Clan}} who inherited ceremonial duties. Her name is said to originate from when her father hunted deer and captured 1,000 deer.
Mote Takami was born with extraordinary spiritual powers, took over his father's duties, carried bow and arrows, followed the Great Deity in hunting and game, and obtained a thousand deer. He had one son and named him Chikatō Kami. Chikatō Kami succeeded in overseeing ceremonial affairs. (Excerpt) Ancient [[Kagura|kagura]] songs: :Chikatō no Kitano Hayashinowa Susumushiwa Susumushiwa Yachiyo no Koyete Tsunenitaisenu :Chikatō no Myōjin Shiyushūreshito Ohosoransu Ohosoransu Yukitataima no Hana no Kiyomeyo Chikatō's shrines are located in the Suwa District's Nakatsusa: Aruga, Uehara, Hanahara, Yokobuki, Kyudo in the Higashichikuma District's Kanda and Hayashi. Situated in {{ill|Urakoyama|ja|宇良古山|lt=Urakoyama}}, there exists a shrine dedicated to him. In ancient times, he was the deity of over thirty villages within the district. His consort is {{ill|Uragokohime-no-Mikoto|ja|宇良古比売命|lt=Uragokohime-no-Mikoto}}. According to oral tradition, there is a shrine of him in the same area. {{ill|Kodama Hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|lt=Kodama Hiko-no-Mikoto}}, the offspring of the Great Deity's child {{ill|Katakura Hade-no-Mikoto|ja|片倉辺命|lt=Katakura Hade-no-Mikoto}}, is considered the fourth generation of the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏|lt=Moriya Clan}}. He married {{ill|Mitasama Hime-no-Kami|ja|美都多麻比売神|lt=Mitasama Hime-no-Kami}}, the child of {{ill|Moritatsu-no-Kami|ja|守達神|lt=Moritatsu-no-Kami}}, and gave birth to {{ill|Hachikushi|ja|八櫛神|lt=Hachikushi}}.Suwa Education Association. "『守矢氏系譜』" 『Suwa Historical Documents Collection, Volume 28』, 1938, pp. 31-72.Nomoto Mikato. "Approach to Chikatō Kami" 『Study on the Emergence and Development of Suwa Faith』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2018, pp. 333-336.Imai Nohiku. "O Suwa-sama Faith and Kami no Naga" 『Gods' Village: Ancient Suwa Stories』, Kokusho Publishing, 1976, pp. 117-118.
According to the 『Genealogy』, Chikatō Kami married {{ill|Uragokohime-no-Mikoto|ja|宇良古比売命|lt=Uragokohime-no-Mikoto}} and moved to {{ill|Urakoyama|ja|宇良古山|lt=Urakoyama}} (now {{ill|Matsumoto City|ja|松本市|lt=Matsumoto City}}, {{ill|Kanda, Matsumoto|ja|神田|lt=Kanda}}), which suggests that Chikatō Kami was either exiled from Suwa or left voluntarily. It is also believed that the succession of Chikatō Kami by {{ill|Take Minakata|ja|建御名方神|lt=Take Minakata}}'s grandchild {{ill|Kodama Hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|lt=Kodama Hiko-no-Mikoto}} indicates that the {{ill|Suwa Clan|ja|諏訪氏|lt=Suwa Clan}} (descendants of the mythological {{ill|Take Minakata|ja|建御名方神|lt=Take Minakata}}) took over the local worship duties of indigenous forces. There are also traditions where the foreign-born Chikatō Kami married local daughters not only in Matsumoto but also in other places. A famous tale narrates that "Chikato Kami" from Shinshu came to {{ill|Akagiyama Shrine|ja|赤城神社|lt=Akagiyama Shrine}} and married the sister of the {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神|lt=Akagi Shrine}}.Imai Nohiku. "Chikato-sama" 『Study on the Emergence and Development of Suwa Faith』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2018, p.325.Nomoto Mikato. "Approach to Chikatō Kami" 『Study on the Emergence and Development of Suwa Faith』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2018, p.342.. == Faith == According to Imai Nohiku's research on the distribution of {{ill|Mishagujishin|ja|ミシャグジ信仰|lt=Mishagujishin}}, Chikatō Kami shrines are found not only in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|lt=Nagano Prefecture}} (13 shrines) but also in {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture}} (8 shrines), {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|lt=Saitama Prefecture}} (12 shrines), {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|lt=Gunma Prefecture}} (20 shrines), {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}} (12 shrines), {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture}} (7 shrines), and {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}} (15 shrines), spreading from Kanto to Tohoku. They have various representations such as "Chikatō," "Chikata," "Chikato," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," "Chikata," and "Chikata," with pronunciations including "Chikata," "Chikatsu," "Chikato (u)," etc.Imai Nohiku. "Chikatō Kami Approach" 『Study on the Emergence and Development of Suwa Faith』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2018, p.326.Nomoto Mikato. "Approach to Chikatō Kami" 『Study on the Emergence and Development of Suwa Faith』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2018, p.343.. The distribution of Chikatō Kami shrines is characterized by their apparent movement along mountainous regions (such as the [[Yatsugatake|八ヶ岳]], [[Haruna Mountain|榛名山]], [[Akagi Mountain|赤城山]], [[Mount Ontake|男体山]], [[Yamigomezaki|八溝山]]) and is believed to have spread through the eastward expansion of hunting clans (descendants of the [[Moriya Clan|守矢族]], the so-called [[Mote Yago|洩矢族]]). As deities of hunting, they are closely related to {{ill|Mishagujishin|ja|ミシャグジ信仰|lt=Mishagujishin}} and {{ill|Tenpaku Faith|ja|天白信仰|lt=Tenpaku Faith}}. According to Imai's research, in Yamanashi, Saitama, and Gunma, {{ill|Mishagujishin|ja|ミシャグジ信仰|lt=Mishagujishin}} shrines coincide with Chikatō Kami shrines.Yamato Iwao. "Shinano Ancient History Study" 『Shinano Ancient History Study』, Meicho Publishing, 1990, pp.201-202.Imai Nohiku. "Survey Collection of Shrine Priests" 『Study on Ancient Suwa and Mishagujis' Ceremonial Systems』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2017, pp.212-281.. In addition to hunting deities from Suwa (Shinshu), there are regions where Chikatō Kami is also revered as a deity of agriculture and fire prevention, similar to {{ill|Mishagujishin|ja|ミシャグジ信仰|lt=Mishagujishin}} and {{ill|Tenpaku Faith|ja|天白信仰|lt=Tenpaku Faith}}, with many shrines worshiping them as {{ill|Ishibō|ja|石棒|lt=Ishibō}} deities.Nomoto Mikato. "Approach to Chikatō Kami" 『Study on the Emergence and Development of Suwa Faith』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2018, pp.347-349.Imai Nohiku. "Survey of Shrine Priests" 『Study on Ancient Suwa and Mishagujis' Ceremonial Systems』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2017, pp.300-301.Imai Nohiku. "Survey of Shrine Priests" 『Study on Ancient Suwa and Mishagujis' Ceremonial Systems』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2017, pp.338-339.Tanaka Moto. "Primitive Agricultural Ritual Elements in Mote Yashima Ceremonial System" 『Study on Ancient Suwa and Mishagujis' Ceremonial Systems』, Ancient Tribe Research Society edited by Jinkan-sha, 2017, p.192.. Moreover, many shrines have different main deities depending on the region, even within {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}. There are still many unexplored shrine precincts and regions, leaving numerous mysteries unresolved. === Main Shrines === ==== Koshin Region ==== * {{ill|Kanda Chikatō Shrine|ja|神田千鹿頭神社|lt=Kanda Chikatō Shrine}}, {{ill|Hayashi Chikatō Shrine|ja|林千鹿頭神社|lt=Hayashi Chikatō Shrine}} ({{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}, {{ill|Matsumoto City|ja|松本市|lt=Matsumoto City}} - Hataiwa) * {{ill|Chikatō Shrine|ja|千鹿頭神社|lt=Chikatō Shrine}} ({{ill|Suwa City, Nagano|ja|諏訪市|lt=Suwa City}}, Toyoda Aruga) * {{ill|Chikatō Shrine|ja|千鹿頭神社|lt=Chikatō Shrine}} ({{ill|Chino City|ja|茅野市|lt=Chino City}}, Chino Uehara) * {{ill|Chikatō Shrine|ja|千鹿頭神社|lt=Chikatō Shrine}} ({{ill|Chino City|ja|茅野市|lt=Chino City}}, Yoneda Hanahara) * {{ill|Chikatō Shrine|ja|千鹿頭神社|lt=Chikatō Shrine}} ({{ill|Yokobuki, Suwa City|ja|横吹|lt=Yokobuki}}, Suwa City) * {{ill|Chikatō Shrine|ja|千鹿頭神社|lt=Chikatō Shrine}} ({{ill|Higashiina District, Ichigata Town|ja|上伊那郡|lt=Higashiina District, Ichigata Town}}, Higashiina District, Ichigata Town) * {{ill|Chikata Shrine|ja|近津神社|lt=Chikata Shrine}} ({{ill|Kofu City, Yamanashi|ja|甲府市|lt=Kofu City}}, Kiyatecho) * {{ill|Chikata Shrine|ja|近戸神社|lt=Chikata Shrine}} ({{ill|Kofu City, Yamanashi|ja|甲府市|lt=Kofu City}}, Takamachi) ==== Kanto Region ==== * {{ill|Oogo Shrine|ja|大胡神社|lt=Oogo Shrine}} (Former {{ill|Chikata Shrine|ja|近戸神社|lt=Chikata Shrine}}) ({{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市|lt=Maebashi City}}, Kawaharaha) * {{ill|Chikata Shrine|ja|近戸神社|lt=Chikata Shrine}} ({{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kasukawa Town|ja|粕川町|lt=Kasukawa Town}}) * {{ill|Chikata Shrine|ja|近戸神社|lt=Chikata Shrine}} ({{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Battoi Town|ja|笂井町|lt=Battoi Town}}) * {{ill|Oyato Shrine|ja|親都神社|lt=Oyato Shrine}} ({{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Agatsuma District, Nakanojō Town|ja|吾妻郡|lt=Agatsuma District, Nakanojō Town}}) * {{ill|Chihō Shrine (Kazo Central)|ja|千方神社}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kazo City|ja|加須市|lt=Kazo City}}) * {{ill|Chishō Shrine (Kuki City's Kamijo)|ja|千勝社}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kuki City|ja|久喜市|lt=Kuki City}} - Kamiji) * {{ill|Chishō Shrine (Kuki City's Honmachi)|ja|千勝神社}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kuki City|ja|久喜市|lt=Kuki City}} - Honmachi) * {{ill|Chishō Shrine (Kuki City's Yoshiba)|ja|千勝神社}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kuki City|ja|久喜市|lt=Kuki City}} - Yoshiba) * {{ill|Chikagata Shrine|ja|千形神社}} ({{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kumagaya City|ja|熊谷市|lt=Kumagaya City}} - Honmachi) * {{ill|Chigata Shrine|ja|智賀都神社}} ({{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya City|ja|宇都宮市|lt=Utsunomiya City}} - Tokujircho) * {{ill|Chihō Shrine|ja|智方神社}} ({{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Oyama City|ja|小山市|lt=Oyama City}} - Takei) * {{ill|Chiha Shrine|ja|血方神社}} ({{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Oyama City|ja|小山市|lt=Oyama City}} - Taman) * {{ill|Chikata Shrine (Kanda)|ja|近津神社}} ({{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Omiya City|ja|久喜市|lt=Omiya City}}) * {{ill|Chishō Shrine (Tsukubaji)|ja|千勝神社}} ({{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Tsukuba City|ja|つくば市|lt=Tsukuba City}} - Tomizaki) ==== Tohoku Region ==== * {{ill|Tudokohachishin Jinja|ja|都々古別神社|lt=Tudokohachishin Jinja}} - {{ill|Suwa District, Tobamachi|ja|東白川郡|lt=Suwa District, Tobamachi}} - Tobamachi * {{ill|Tudokohachishin Jinja|ja|都々古別神社|lt=Tudokohachishin Jinja}} ({{ill|Suwa District, Tashitsuki|ja|東白川郡棚倉町八槻|lt=Suwa District, Tashitsuki}}) - {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Iwanose Shrine|ja|岩峰山石神社|lt=Iwanose Shrine}} * {{ill|Hisayose Shrine (Chihashin Shrine)|ja|久来石神社}} ({{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}} - Hikoshi) == Footnotes == == References == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=『信州の文化シリーズ 寺と神社』|year=1981|publisher=Shinshu Mainichi Shimbunsha}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=『探訪 信州の古寺 天台宗・真言宗』|year=1996|publisher=Kyodo Publishing}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=『Shuya Clan Genealogy』|year=2017|editor=Ancient Tribe Research Society|publisher=Ningen-sha|isbn=978-4-908-62717-0|ref={{Harvid|Shuya Clan Genealogy|2017年}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=『Shinano Ancient History Study』|year=1990|editor=Yamato Iwao|publisher=Meicho Publishing|isbn=978-4-626-01363-7|ref={{Harvid|Shinano Ancient History Study|1990年}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=『Study on Ancient Suwa and Mishagujis' Ceremonial Systems』|year=2017|editor=Ancient Tribe Research Society|publisher=Ningen-sha|isbn=978-4-908-62716-3|ref={{Harvid|Study on Ancient Suwa and Mishagujis' Ceremonial Systems|2017年}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=『Study on Ancient Suwa and Mishagujis' Ceremonial Systems』|year=2017|editor=Ancient Tribe Research Society|publisher=Ningen-sha|isbn=978-4-908-62717-0|ref={{Harvid|Study on Ancient Suwa and Mishagujis' Ceremonial Systems|2018年}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=『Study on Mishagujishin』|year=2017|editor=Swanimism|title_addition= Issue 3 Special Feature: Mishaguji Restart}} == Related Topics == * [[List of Japanese deities|List of Japanese deities]] {{Shinto Navbox}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyatsuhime Tsumatsuhime}} [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Goddesses of vegetation]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Shinto goddesses]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[category:original pages]] [[ja:オオヤツヒメ・ツマツヒメ]] {{User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru Faith}}

'''Ōyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime''' are two {{ill|Kami|ja|神道の神|lt=Shinto deities}} appearing in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythological]] [[deity (Shinto)|female deities]]. In one book of the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], they are recorded as the daughters of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊|lt=Susanoo}} (father) and {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Isonotakeru no mikoto|ja|五十猛命}}'''|五十猛命|}} (brother). Situated in {{ill|Urakoyama|ja|宇良古山|lt=Urakoyama}}, there exists a shrine dedicated to him. In ancient times, he was the deity of over thirty villages within the district. His consort is {{ill|Uragokohime-no-Mikoto|ja|宇良古比売命|lt=Uragokohime-no-Mikoto}}.
